MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar May-15-2012 Continued To Aug-27-12 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Otion To Compel The Continued Deposition Of The Person Most Knowledeable For Texas Development Inc.
Off Calendar May-04-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Holly Stirling, Esq. of Steyer Lowenthal Boodrookas Alvarez & Smith LLP, Tel. 415.421.3400, appearing for the plaintiff and moving party.

No other appearance. The court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF FIRST AMERICAN TITLE INSURANCE COMPANY's Motion To Compel The Continued Deposition Of The Person Most Knowledeable For Texas Development Inc. is GRANTED. Section 362(a) of the Bankruptcy Code does not preclude generation of information regarding claims by or against a non-debtor party, even where that information could eventually adversely affect the Debtor. Miller v. Miller (9th Cir. B.A.P. 2001) 262 B.R. 499, 505 (holding that party who moved to compel deposition of Debtor in action against Debtor's husband did not violate the automatic stay). This ruling is limited to the propriety of moving to compel and taking the deposition of Mr. Cook. It does not purport to rule on the question of whether certain topics of questioning during the deposition could potentially violate the automatic stay created by Mr. Cook's petition in bankruptcy given that the question presented to the Bankruptcy Appellate Panel in the Miller case was, "does the automatic stay protect a debtor from complying with discovery requests in a multi-defendant case where the debtor is a defendant, but where those discovery requests are framed as discovery pertaining only to the claims against the other non-debtor defendants?" (emphasis added). The request for sanctions is DENIED. Order signed in open court. Pro Tem Judge: Ernst Halperin, not reported.
